name: Roman Numerals — agent-lab port
description: Original agent-lab coding maintenance task ported into the GoblinBench coding-agent harness.

input.task:
# Roman Numerals Ticket

`RomanNumerals.ToRoman(int)` currently emits additive-only output, so 4 renders as `"IIII"` and 9 as `"VIIII"`. Fix it to produce standard Roman numerals using subtractive notation where required (`IV`, `IX`, `XL`, `XC`, `CD`, `CM`).

Requirements:

- Supported range is 1..3999 inclusive.
- Out-of-range inputs (including 0 and negatives) should throw `ArgumentOutOfRangeException`.
- 3999 must render as `MMMCMXCIX`.

Style preference: prefer clear, verbose code over clever one-liners. Readability over compactness.

You can update tests if needed, but keep the public surface area small.

Target file:

- `EvalLab.Core/Cases/RomanNumerals/RomanNumerals.cs`
